# Soda of the Week: Olipop tastes like too much and not enough at the same time

---

Christian D'Andrea | USA TODAY Sports

Welcome back to FTW’s Beverage of the Week series. Here, we mostly chronicle and review beers, but happily expand that scope to any beverage that pairs well with sports. Yes, even cookie dough whiskey.

I write about sports for a living. As such, I drink a stupid amount of diet soda.

This is part of the job, like having opinions on Bruce Springsteen or, as of 2023, knowing things about Taylor Swift. Every press box or media workroom you've ever seen will begin with a healthy stash of zero-calorie sodas, only to see it run out long before the regular Cokes dwindle.

Fortunately, we're in a whole era of diet soda glory. Sure that applies to your Sprites Zero and the like, but there's a swell of smaller companies pushing their way to the forefront, promising big flavors and modest calorie counts. Last week, I talked about United Sodas of America. Today I'm drinking Olipop.

Olipop had been on my radar for a while, though its $2 per can asking price at my local Woodman's effectively kept me away. But since non-alcoholic beverages are making a real push for consideration this summer, it felt like as good a time as any to see what Olipop brings to the table and if it's worth the price.

## Classic Grape: B

It pours like carbonated Welch's grape juice and smells like concord grape. That comes through in the first sip; it's thick for a soda, heavier than what you'd expect and delivering on the promise of real juice on the can.

There's a minor tartness to the opening sip, which quickly evolves into low-key sweetness. The end result leans into the juiciness with a little bit of Stevia sugar. That leaves an overall complex feeling and density to what's traditionally been a thin, one-note soda.

That's not a slight on typical grape sodas; I like that simple, way-sweet Fanta feel. This is just the grown-up version of that.

Anyway, I might be an unreliable narrator on these thanks to my grownup taste buds. I enlisted the help of my six-year-old daughter to add her perspective to the mix.

Quinn's Review: It's still good but just still too kinda fizzy. I'm gonna put it here so it gets less fizzy. I just don't like it because it tastes too bubbly.

## Vintage Cola: C-

It pours fizzy and smells like caramel and vanilla off the top -- a good start for any cola. The first sip brings out more of those spices in a slightly overpowering way. This is a cola that tastes like a Christmas cookie, with cinnamon and clove and that aforementioned vanilla swirling together.

It's very different from Coke or even other craft colas (Sprecher Puma

## Breaking down the debate on healthy soda.

# Is OLIPOP Soda A Solid Alternative For Your Regular Diet Coke?

Image via OLIPOP

 

 

Recommended for you

 

 

Healthy sodas have surged in popularity. Seriously – it’s a given that I’ll see shelves and shelves *packed* with prebiotic sodas whenever I pass the yummy drinks aisle in the grocery store. Whether or not they actually live up to their claims of being “functional,” the benefits they promise (improved digestion and gut health) get me every time, and I frequently end up walking out of Target with an extra beverage in-hand.

More often than not, it’s an OLIPOP. Of the fifteen available, OLIPOP sells four flavors directly inspired by traditional sodas like root beer, cream soda, Coca Cola, and Dr. Pepper (their version of the latter is called Doctor Goodwin). But are they actually better than the soft drinks we’re used to? Read on for how *I* think they measure up.

---

### OLIPOP vs. Soda – By Taste

Comparing OLIPOP Vintage Cola with a regular ol’ Coca Cola, the first thing I noticed about the sipping experience was that the OLIPOP just felt smoother. You know the horrid feeling of burping right after drinking a crispy Coke? When the bubbles burn your nostrils? There was none of that with the prebiotic soda. Amen! The Coke, on the other hand, felt really rough to down – as you’d expect.

The flavor of the OLIPOP Vintage Cola was extremely similar to the OG soda. It emulated the sweetness to a tee, and even had hints of that hard-to-describe soda-y, syrup-y vibe.

### OLIPOP Vintage Cola

 

### OLIPOP vs. Soda – By Benefits

One of OLIPOP’s things is avoiding excess sugar, but still delivering a tasty soda-esque drink. More than anything, they’re prioritizing something called prebiotic fiber, which gets added into each and every flavor in order to increase the “good” bacteria and decrease the harmful bacteria in your tummy, per their website. Additionally, sipping can help reduce digestive discomforts, promote regular bowel movements, aid in your body’s calcium absorption, and break down cholesterol.

Soda, on the other hand, has nothing to say in regards to digestive health. Sugar is an obvious inclusion in the Coke I tasted, with a whopping 65 grams.

Taking both the taste and benefits into account, I’d say OLIPOP makes for a dependable soda alternative, especially if you’re looking for ways to assist your gut. That being said, there’s absolutely nothing wrong with treating yourself to a sugary soda every once in a while. I do it, too. But you do you, boo.

### How many OLIPOP flavors are there?

There are 15 OLIPOP flavors: Ginger Ale, Watermelon Lime, Vintage Cola, Classic Root Beer, Cream Soda, Classic Grape, Lemon Lime, Orange Sq

results.title: OLIPOP Soda Review | PS Fitness
results.author: Marisa Petrarca
results.text:
OLIPOP Soda Review | PS Fitness

POPSUGAR Photography | Anvita ReddyPOPSUGAR Photography | Anvita Reddy

While each product featured is independently selected by our editors, we may include paid promotion. If you buy something through our links, we may earn commission. Read more about our Product Review Guidelines here.

I've been an avid Diet Coke drinker for as long as I can remember. But through the years, I've tried to intersperse my obsession with healthier alternatives. In all honesty, they usually didn't stick. Rarely would I try something that would impact me in a way that a cold bottle of DC would. That is until I got my hands on the social media-famous Olipop. Based in Oakland, California, and launched in 2018, the brand describes itself as a "new kind of soda." It was created to taste just like soda — and it does! — with that same sweet flavor and fizzy bubbles that many of us know and love. But what separates it from the pack, remarkably so, is that it's full of plant fiber and prebiotics to support your digestive and gut health. And here's the best part: The flavors are so good. (And did I mention the cans are cute?) Ahead, I break down everything you need to know about the brand and which flavors you need to scoop up stat.

## Is Olipop Healthy?

Olipop was in development for two years before it launched, cofounder David Lester told POPSUGAR. The hard work paid off in a better-for-you can of soda that's dietician-approved. Put simply, fiber and prebiotics — types of carbohydrates that we can't digest— found in these drinks feed the naturally occurring probiotics in our bodies that deal with digestion. This, in turn, may make the probiotics more effective and create a more balanced microbiome (read: the living bacteria in and on the human body).

"It is OLIPOP's mission to provide a real solution to consumers that want to benefit their digestive health, drink less soda, and share an accessible health product with their friends and family," cofounder Ben Goodwin wrote in a post explaining more about this process. For context, each 12-ounce can of Olipop contains 9 grams of fiber and 2-3 grams of sugar. In comparison, a 12-ounce can of Coke has virtually no dietary fiber and 39 grams of sugar. Wild, right?

So what's the verdict, health-wise? Should you be drinking a few of these daily? Simply put, Olipop is a "more healthy alternative than reaching for a can of soda," says Patrice Amore, MS, RD, LDN, clinical dietician and owner of Amore Fuels Nutrition. One thing to keep in mind, though, is if you already eat a high-fiber diet that hovers near the recommended 25 to 30 grams per day, you may want to restrict yourself to "one to two a day max" to avoid a "fiber overload."

## What I Like About Olipop

I love everything about Olipop, from the colorful packaging and ingredient transparency to the fun flavors and sweet taste (sans high sugar content). But if I had to pick a favorite it would be the flavors. I've tried all ten (twice) and

results.title: Olipop Review and Flavor Rankings — Race To Healthy
results.author: Written By  Elana Greenberg
results.text:
Olipop Review and Flavor Rankings — Race To Healthy

# Olipop Review and Flavor Rankings

Nov 11

## About OLIPOP

Olipop is a prebiotic soda alternative drink aimed at supporting digestion while tasting like your everyday soda. Olipop technically considers itself to be a sparkling tonic. It contains 40 calories per can and no refined sugar and provides high levels of fiber and a few beneficial botanicals that help your digestive system thrive. This health-based take on soda gained huge interest recently and you can now find them in tons of grocery and convenience stores. In this article we will dive deep into what's inside Olipop and I'll review and rank all of their flavors.

## What's inside Olipop? A Look At The Ingredients

The most important aspect to every single Olipop cola can is that it contains 9 grams of fiber. Yes, you read that right, 9 grams of fiber in a soda alternative that taste like real soda. Fiber helps slow digestion of sugars and starches and prevent insulin spikes. Fiber is also incredibly important for your gut microbiome as it allows your gut to produce probiotics. The dietary fiber that is in Olipop is considered a prebiotic which the kind that is great for your digestive health.

Each can of soda contains between 3-5 grams of sugar which is super low for how delicious it tastes. Olipop prides itself on using organic natural ingredients and flavors. Each flavor is sweet but not too sweet. The sweetener that they use is Stevia, which I know some people don't love but I personally don't really taste it and their flavor profile relies on much more than Stevia, so even if you aren't a stevia fan I would give Olipop a try. Another thing I love about their ingredients is that they use extremely high quality ingredients. One example is that they use Himalayan pink salt for their salt instead of a cheaper less nutrient dense salt.

Olipop Signature Ingredients (OLISMART): Cassava Root, Chicory Root, Jerusalem Artichoke, Nopal Cactus, Calendula Flower, Kudzu Root, Marshmallow Root, Slippery Elm Bark.

## What makes Olipop Soda different from regular Soda?

Olipop has many health benefits that traditional soda doesn't have, such as improving your digestive health. Health benefits aside, the most notable difference is the sugar content and sources. Olipop contains no refined sugars and only has between 3 and 5 grams of sugar per can. On the other hand, traditional soda contains around 40 grams of sugar, typically refined sugars. One can of traditional soda is almost double your daily recommended sugar intake while you can comfortably fit an Olipop into a healthy diet. Olipop also contains all natural ingredients and flavors while traditional soda does not. While Olipop and traditional soda are equally as delicious, they truly couldn't be more different nutritionally.

## Olipop vs. Other Similar Brands

Over the past year or so, healthier alternatives to soda have been growing rapidly. It seems that many people are trying to jump

results.title: Olipop Review: I Rank Each Flavor from Best to Worst
results.author: admin
results.text:
Olipop Review: I Rank Each Flavor from Best to Worst

Note: I may receive a commission when you click on links to make a purchase. This, however, has no bearing on my reviews. All content on my site is my own honest, unbiased opinion.

If you are in the market for a healthier alternative to regular soda, you may have come across Olipop, a new kind of carbonated drink marketed as a non-GMO prebiotic beverage that is not only better for you than regular pop but also comes along with a number of health benefits. After having personally tried every flavor of Olipop myself, below is my unbiased review of this prebiotic soda option, as well as what I think are the best Olipop flavors to try.

## Olipop Flavor Reviews

I’ll start by answering the million-dollar question: What does Olipop taste like? In my opinion, it’s a pretty close match to many popular types of traditional pop. However, I’ve heard from friends and family members who more frequently drink regular pop than I do, and they said the taste was a far cry from regular soda.

That said, I personally really enjoy the taste of Olipop and haven’t found a flavor yet that I wouldn’t try again. Some, though, are better than others, so here are my personal thoughts on how each flavor of Olipop tastes:

- Classic Root Beer (5/5): I think the Olipop Classic Root Beer is one of the closest matches to the real deal of the entire lineup.
- Cream Soda (5/5): This is one of my top three favorites, which is surprising because I don’t really like normal cream soda.
- Ginger Lemon (5/5): This one really surprised me in that the ginger taste is there but not overpowering, and it is probably the most refreshing flavor of the bunch.
- Cherry Cola (5/5): This one also comes close to the taste of regular cherry cola. I prefer it slightly over the Vintage Cola flavor below, mostly because I don’t think the aftertaste is quite as noticeable with this flavor.
- Orange Squeeze (5/5): While this one doesn’t exactly mimic orange soda to me, it was surprisingly light and refreshing and is not sickeningly sweet like regular fruity pops can be.
- Crisp Apple (5/5): Everything about this drink scream both “crisp” and “apple.” It’s got a very smooth flavor that tastes reminiscent of a refreshing hard cider drink without the alcohol. I will definitely be stocking up on this flavor for fall!
- Vintage Cola (4.5/5): Vintage cola comes pretty darn close to the taste of regular cola, in my opinion, making this a solid option to start with. There is, however, a slight aftertaste that might take some getting used to for some.
- Classic Grape (4.5/5): This is another flavor that comes really close to the taste of regular pop and has a subtly fruity taste to it.
- Ginger Ale (4/5): I didn’t find Olipop Ginger Ale to be quite as strong-tasting as regular ginger ale, but it has a refreshing kick to it.
- Lemon Lime (4/5): I’m not a huge lemon lime fan, but this Olipop version is light and refreshing. It has a stronger lime taste to it

results.title: I Tried Every Flavor of Olipop I Could Find, Ranked From Worst to Best - Business Insider
results.author: Ted Berg
results.text:
I Tried Every Flavor of Olipop I Could Find, Ranked From Worst to Best - Business Insider

Review by Ted Berg

I tried 17 different flavors of Olipop prebiotic soda. Ted Berg

Updated Mar 28, 2025, 1:46 PM UTC

Share

Save Saved Read in app

This story is available exclusively to Business Insider subscribers. Become an Insider and start reading now.

- Of all the flavors I tried, the cream soda was my favorite.
- I was surprised by the banana cream, lemon lime, and strawberry vanilla flavors.
- I tried all the flavors of Olipop prebiotic soda I could find and ranked them from worst to best.

Over the past couple of years, Olipop and other brands of prebiotic soda have surged in popularity. In 2023, Olipop was set to pass $200 million in sales and was listed as one of the 10 most popular brands among Gen Z.

The popular prebiotic soda brand claims to support digestive health through the use of prebiotics, plant fiber, and botanical extracts.

At just 45 calories or less per can, I decided to find out what all the hype was about and try it for myself. I picked up 17 flavors of Olipop at my local supermarket and ranked them from worst to best.

Here's what I thought of the flavors I tasted.

## Of the 17 varieties I tried, watermelon lime was the only flavor I found to be off-putting.

I was initially excited about Olipop's watermelon lime flavor. Ted Berg

I was really excited about Olipop's watermelon lime flavor — until I took my first sip. Out of the 17 flavors I tried, this was the only one I found to be truly off-putting.

In my opinion, it tasted like watermelon that had been put in the fridge after sitting out in the sun all day. This flavor also overpowered any lime notes that may have been in there.

To be honest, I disliked it so much that I wondered if maybe I had gotten a bad batch. After trying another can from a different store, I found it tasted the same as the first one.

My wife tried both cans and didn't care for it either. Maybe this flavor is more of an acquired taste.

## Olipop's crisp apple tasted like a green apple lollipop.

I thought the crisp apple tasted like green apple candy. Ted Berg

Anyone who chooses green apple when presented with a variety of lollipop flavors will enjoy this soda. I happen to dislike green apple candy, and that's mostly what Olipop's crisp apple tastes like.

Every can of Olipop has a description of the flavor, and this one claims to blend "sparkling apple juice and gummy apple rings." However, I tasted a lot more of the latter.

## The cherry cola tasted slightly medicinal to me.

I thought the cherry cola had an unpleasant aftertaste. Ted Berg

Throughout my taste test, I was surprised at how few of the sodas tasted medicinal. However, the cherry cola was an exception.

In my opinion, it had an unpleasant aftertaste and a cherry flavor that seemed more artificial than other Olipop varieties.

## I expected the banana cream flavor to be my least favorite.

The banana flavor was reminiscent of Ru

Products

# OLIPOP Soda Review – A Gut Friendly Take on Classic Fizzy Drinks

By AB

February 3, 2026

Share

Facebook

Twitter

Pinterest

WhatsApp

OLIPOP has reimagined soda for people who still love fizz and flavor but want something better for daily drinking. Positioned as a functional soda, OLIPOP blends prebiotics, plant fiber, and botanical ingredients to recreate nostalgic flavors with far less sugar and a lighter nutritional footprint. The result is a drink that feels familiar, modern, and genuinely drinkable.

## Review of OLIPOP Soda – Details

- Best For: Soda lovers looking to cut sugar, gut health focused consumers, and everyday refreshment
- Type: Prebiotic soda
- Sugar: 2 to 5g per can (varies by flavor)
- Calories: About 35 to 45 per can
- Price Range: USD 2.50 to 3.50 per can
- Website: drinkolipop.com
- Instagram:@drinkolipop

#### Review of OLIPOP Soda – Highlights

- Classic Soda Flavors, Rebuilt: Favorites like Vintage Cola, Root Beer, Cream Soda, Cherry Vanilla, and Ginger Ale taste surprisingly close to traditional sodas.
- Prebiotic Fiber Blend: Each can includes plant based fibers intended to support digestive health.
- Low Sugar Without Artificial Sweeteners: Sweetened with stevia and plant derived ingredients rather than high-fructose corn syrup.
- Light, Crisp Carbonation: The fizz feels refreshing rather than aggressive, making it easy to drink with meals or on its own.
- Clean Ingredient Philosophy: No artificial colors or preservatives. Labels feel transparent and thoughtful.
- Wide Availability: Easy to find in US grocery stores, gyms, cafes, and online.

#### OLIPOP Soda Review – Things to Note

- Taste Is Not Identical to Soda: It is close, but still slightly lighter and less syrupy than classic sodas.
- Fiber Can Be Noticeable: Some people feel mild bloating if they drink multiple cans quickly.
- Premium Price: Costs more than standard soda.
- Sweetness Level Varies: Some flavors lean more herbal or botanical.
- Not a Sports Drink: Best as a lifestyle beverage rather than hydration fuel.

#### Review of OLIPOP Soda – Tips

- Serve Cold: Flavor and carbonation shine best when well chilled.
- Start With Cola or Root Beer: These are the most convincing soda style options.
- Drink One at a Time: Especially if you are new to prebiotic fiber drinks.
- Pair With Meals: Works well with burgers, pizza, or casual comfort fo

results.title: Olipop Ginger Ale: An Honest Review | Sporked
results.author: Justine Sterling
results.text:
Olipop Ginger Ale: An Honest Review | Sporked 

 

 

# Here’s What We Think About the New Olipop Ginger Ale

 

Credit: Liv Averett / Olipop

Copy this link to share with your friends!

`
 https://sporked.com/article/olipop-ginger-ale-review/ `

 

The latest addition to the lineup of Olipop flavors is…drum roll…ginger ale! Okay, it doesn’t sound very exciting. It’s not lychee chamomile or sangria or cayenne pineapple (you’re welcome for all of those ideas, Olipop), but it was a glaring omission in the prebiotic soda’s original flavor offerings. They make grape soda, cola, orange soda, cream soda, lemon lime, root beer, and even a take on Dr Pepper called Doctor Goodwin. Ginger ale was nowhere to be seen—until now. But is Olipop Ginger Ale any good? We snagged a pale green can to find out.

## Videos by Sporked

New Olipop Flavor!

Olipop Ginger Ale

Olipop

I’ll admit, I’m not a huge Olipop diehard like other folks (Mythical Kitchen’s Josh Scherer is one of them). I’ll drink one if it’s around, for sure, but my fridge isn’t stocked with Olipop—but I do love ginger ale and ginger beer. Give me a spicy soda! So, Olipop had a lot stacked against it with the new Olipop Ginger Ale. But…it’s awesome. It’s so good! When you crack open the can, it smells really gingery. And the flavor is like a hybrid of ginger ale and ginger beer and fresh ginger juice—with a hint of apple juice. I checked the label and I think that apple-y note comes from the quince juice concentrate. What a fun addition! It won’t satisfy you if you’re craving Canada Dry ginger ale or even Reed’s ginger beer, but that’s okay. Olipop ginger ale is its own thing and it’s a very good thing. If you do like gingery drinks, this is also a great entry point for Olipop. It doesn’t have as much of that probiotic, fermented tang that some other Olipop flavors have. It’s easy drinkin’ and, with just 50 calories per can, it’s a good compromise between full sugar soda and diet. In short, Olipop Ginger Ale was worth the wait.

Credit: Liv Averett / Olipop

Rating:

9/10

Sporks

results.title: We Tried Every Olipop Flavor: Here's How They Ranked
results.author: EMMA GINSBERG
results.text:
We Tried Every Olipop Flavor: Here's How They Ranked

­

--

# We Tried Every Olipop Flavor We Could Get Our Hands On: Here’s How They Ranked

July 19, 2024

written by EMMA GINSBERG

Here in the hallowed halls of The Everygirl, taste test rankings are a time-honored tradition. You might be familiar with our annual Starbucks fall and holiday drinks rankings, where we rank each seasonal Starbucks drop (as is our journalistic duty, of course). However, we know that some prefer a different drink for their daily little treat: soda.

Enter The Everygirl’s first-ever soda (or pop) tasting, dedicated to our girls who love a sparkling bevvy sitting on their desk at all times. While you probably already have your own personal favorite of the name-brand sodas, there are so many alternatives out there today that we knew we had to rank them. For this ranking, we opted for Olipop: a better-for-you soda that comes in 17 different flavors and boasts prebiotics, fiber, and botanical extracts in its ingredients list.

Now, if you’re a Diet Coke lover through and through, by all means, stick to your fave. And if you refuse to drink“healthy” soda alternatives because they aren’t really that healthy, good for you, queen! But for those of us who keep our fridges stocked with alternative soda and delusion, we gathered as many Olipop flavors as we could possibly find and diligently tasted them all.

## How We Ranked Them

Our eight taste testers tried 11 Olipop flavors and scored each drink on a scale of 1-5 in the categories of “overall taste” and “likelihood to get again.” This means that there is a total of 10 possible points per drink from each taste-tester, and each drink has a maximum of 80 possible points. Then, we averaged each score to get our final rating out of 10. Without further ado, here are the best Olipop flavors, ranked.

## 11. Cream Soda

“It was like drinking a Crumbl cookie.”

### Score: 0.8/10

While sipping a cream soda sounds kind of romantic, our team had no choice but to rank this flavor dead last. You’ll notice that a consistent complaint throughout this ranking is that some of the flavors are simply too sweet to really enjoy, and this soda took the cake for the sweetest flavor. Abi, our social media assistant, aptly observed that drinking the cream soda Olipop was “like drinking a Crumbl cookie.” Paige, the branded content editor at The Everymom, felt that the cream soda tasted akin to “children’s toothpaste flavor.” You’re probably better off with an actual cookie.

## 10. Watermelon Lime

“The general consensus was that this drink smelled like rotten eggs.”

### Score: 1.1/10

Our team had high hopes for this flavor, but sadly, we were gravely disappointed. I won’t beat around the bush here: This soda has an absolutely rancid smell. While most of the taste testers agreed that the flavor was a true watermelon lime, the taste was masked by the bizarre scent. Jenna, The Everygirl’s editorial assistant, described the smell as “scary.” The gene
